policy.source_text_logo_rule: Reject or crop/clean any source with unavoidable non-caption text, logos, watermarks, scale bars, microscope UI, article labels.

download_status.result: All 10 previously captured right-safe/open candidates are now present locally. C002 was verified with ffprobe metadata as a CC BY 4.0 PLOS/Commons microscope video explicitly covering stages I active hydrated, II dehydrating/tucking in, III anhydrobiotic tun state, and IV rehydration.

beat_01_active_baseline.need: Hydrated/active tardigrade, recognizable barrel body, legs/claws, microscope lighting.

beat_02_desiccation_into_tun.need: Hydrated-to-contracted/tun transition or paired active/tun real plates.

beat_03_dormant_survival_pause.need: Still tun-state tardigrade; no internal/HUD graphics.

beat_04_water_reactivation.need: Water returning plus credible rehydrating/resuming movement.
beat_04_water_reactivation.coverage_warning: Updated 2026-06-06: C002 is a right-safe CC BY 4.0 PLOS/Commons microscope video whose embedded metadata explicitly states the sequence includes stage IV rehydration after tun formation, so it is the best acquired rehydration/reactivation source. Precise remaining gap: no separate open/free zero-text source was found that clearly shows the water droplet/meniscus/flow returning in-frame. If Gate B requires visible water-return, use the safe fallback: cut from verified tun state (C002/C004) to verified active/post-water state (C002/C007/C001/C005/C006) only with script/visual wording changed away from showing literal water flow.

beat_05_final_payoff_hero.need: Best clean, credible hydrated hero tardigrade microscope/macro shot.

candidates.title: BeautifulTardigrade.png
candidates.author_or_source: Wikimedia Commons contributor page shown as User:Dora... in API result; verify exact attribution on file page before final use.
candidates.qc_notes: Likely good hero/active microscope candidate. Must inspect for baked-in text, scale bar, watermark, and crop safety after download. Exact author string needs confirmation due rate-limit truncation.

candidates.title: Desiccation-Tolerance-in-the-Tardigrade-Richtersius-coronifer-Relies-on-Muscle-Mediated-Structural-pone.0085091.s001.ogv
candidates.author_or_source: Halberg K, Jørgensen A, Møbjerg N; PLOS ONE supplemental movie for desiccation tolerance in Richtersius coronifer.
beat_fit: verified rehydration/reactivation sequence (metadata stage IV rehydration)
candidates.qc_notes: Best acquired Beat 04 source. Local ffprobe metadata confirms: "Entrance into and exit out of anhydrobiosis can be divided into four separate stages (I, active hydrated; II, dehydrating, tucking in; III, anhydrobiotic tun state; IV, rehydration)." Right-safe as CC BY 4.0 PLOS/Commons. Probe frames near 58/65/72s show microscope imagery without obvious baked-in title/text in the sampled frames, but final crop/QC must still verify no scale/UI/text before plate approval. Does not independently satisfy a strict visible water droplet/meniscus/flow requirement unless that is apparent in selected frames.

candidates.title: SEM image of Milnesium tardigradum in active state - journal.pone.0045682.g001-2.png
candidates.source_platform: Wikimedia Commons / PLOS ONE figure derivative
candidates.author_or_source: Schokraie E, Warnken U, Hotz-Wagenblatt A, Grohme MA, Hengherr S, et al.; PLOS ONE.
candidates.qc_notes: Credible SEM active-state plate; less ideal for live hydrated baseline because SEM is not water/motion, but strong anatomy reference and before/after pair with C004. Check/crop any figure label/scale bar.

candidates.title: SEM image of Milnesium tardigradum in tun state - journal.pone.0045682.g001-3.png
candidates.source_platform: Wikimedia Commons / PLOS ONE figure derivative
candidates.author_or_source: Schokraie E, Warnken U, Hotz-Wagenblatt A, Grohme MA, Hengherr S, et al.; PLOS ONE.
candidates.qc_notes: Strongest known tun-state still candidate. Must crop away labels/scale bars if present. SEM texture is credible but not hydrated microscope style; suitable for Beat 02/03 if WAYS accepts SEM.

candidates.title: Tardigrade Acutuncus Antarcticus.jpg
candidates.qc_notes: Potential credible microscope/macro hero. Must inspect for text/scale/watermark and 9:16 crop safety.

candidates.title: Tardigrade under microscope.jpg
candidates.author_or_source: Wikimedia Commons contributor page shown as User:DSpa... in API result; verify exact attribution on file page before final use.
candidates.qc_notes: Likely useful active microscope shot. Need exact author verification and text/scale QC after download.

candidates.title: Tardigrade under a microscope, from a Scottish high school playing field.webm
candidates.author_or_source: Wikimedia Commons contributor page shown as User:WeAr... in API result; CC0 dedication. Verify exact source page before final use.
candidates.qc_notes: Very useful because it is actual microscope video and CC0. Does not by itself prove water returning; use only as active/reactivated movement unless paired with true water-return source.

candidates.title: FeineFauna different-state-of-tardigrades 01.jpg
candidates.qc_notes: Search result says different states; inspect carefully. If it contains labels or diagram-style text, use only as provenance/reference or crop clean portions. Reject if illustration/cartoon rather than microscope/source-supported.

candidates.title: FeineFauna different-state-of-tardigrades 02.png
candidates.qc_notes: Inspect before use; likely has state labels/graphics. If not real microscope/macro or has baked-in text, do not approve as final plate.

candidates.title: 41598 2019 56965 Fig1 HTML.webp
candidates.source_platform: Wikimedia Commons / Scientific Reports open-access figure
candidates.author_or_source: Ricardo Cardoso Neves, Lykke K. B. Hvidepil, Thomas L. Sørensen, et al.; Scientific Reports 2019 article figure.
candidates.qc_notes: Likely a multi-panel scientific figure; treat as candidate/source-reference only until cropped. Any panel letters, axes, labels, or scale bars must be removed/cropped for final plates.

gate_assessment.gate_A_source_provenance: PASS: 10 open/free candidates have URLs, licenses, source/author notes, and local files; C002 rehydration provenance verified from embedded PLOS/Commons metadata.
gate_assessment.gate_B_say_dog_see_dog: PARTIAL_PASS_WITH_PRECISE_GAP: C002 supports real rehydration/reactivation (stage IV rehydration) and can cover Beat 04 if source-supported re-expansion/resuming movement is accepted. Remaining blocker only if strict plate requires visible water returning as droplet/meniscus/flow in-frame; no better open/free zero-text source with that exact water-return moment was found.
gate_assessment.gate_C_anatomy_QC: PARTIAL: C002 probe frames and existing video frame sheet support microscope tardigrade sequence; full frame/crop QC still needed before final plate approval.
gate_assessment.gate_D_text_QC: PARTIAL: sampled C002 rehydration-region frames showed no obvious baked-in title/text in analysis thumbnails; full selected-frame/crop text QC still required.

next_actions_before_plate_gate: For Beat 04, prioritize C002 stage IV rehydration frames/segment because it is the best right-safe source acquired.
During plate QC, select C002 frames that show re-expansion/resumed movement and verify zero text/logos/UI/scale bars in the actual crop.

If visible water droplet/meniscus/flow cannot be found in C002, do not overclaim a literal water-return shot; change the visual/script beat to a truthful before/after: tun state, then active post-water/reactivated state.
Do not use paid/credit lanes or AI-generated final tardigrade plates.
